title: 601. Which Part of the Indian Constitution contains the Fundamental Rights?
hint: It comes after the Directive Principles chapter.
summary: Fundamental Rights are contained in Part III of the Constitution.

title: 602. Fundamental Rights are provided under Articles:
hint: Starts with Article 12.
summary: Fundamental Rights are covered under Articles 12–35.

title: 603. Which Article guarantees Equality before Law?
hint: It is the first Article under the Right to Equality.
summary: Article 14 guarantees Equality before Law and Equal Protection of Laws.

title: 604. Which Article prohibits discrimination on grounds of religion, race, caste, sex or place of birth?
hint: It comes immediately after Article 14.
summary: Article 15 prohibits discrimination on specified grounds.

title: 605. Equality of opportunity in public employment is guaranteed under:
hint: Think government jobs.
summary: Article 16 provides equality of opportunity in public employment.

title: 606. Which Article abolishes Untouchability?
hint: It follows Article 16.
summary: Article 17 abolishes Untouchability.

title: 607. Which Article abolishes titles except military and academic distinctions?
hint: It is the last Article under the Right to Equality.
summary: Article 18 abolishes titles.

title: 608. Which Fundamental Right includes freedom of speech and expression?
hint: It begins with Article 19.
summary: Freedom of speech and expression is guaranteed under Article 19(1)(a).

title: 609. Which of the following is NOT a freedom guaranteed under Article 19?
hint: Think about the Right to Property.
summary: The Right to Property is a legal right under Article 300A, not a Fundamental Right.

options.title: Freedom to reside anywhere in India

title: 610. Which Fundamental Right was removed by the 44th Constitutional Amendment?
hint: It is now a legal right under Article 300A.
summary: The Right to Property ceased to be a Fundamental Right after the 44th Amendment.

title: 611. Protection in respect of conviction for offences is guaranteed under which Article?
hint: It comes immediately after Article 19.
summary: Article 20 provides protection against ex post facto laws, double jeopardy and self-incrimination.
hint: Think about criminal law safeguards.

title: 612. Article 21 guarantees which Fundamental Right?
summary: Article 21 guarantees Protection of Life and Personal Liberty.
hint: It is one of the most widely interpreted Articles.

options.title: Protection of Life and Personal Liberty

title: 613. Which Article provides the Right to Education for children aged 6–14 years?
summary: Article 21A makes education a Fundamental Right.
hint: Added by the 86th Constitutional Amendment.

title: 614. Which Article protects against arbitrary arrest and detention?
summary: Article 22 provides safeguards against arrest and detention.
hint: It follows Article 21A.

title: 615. Which Article prohibits human trafficking and forced labour?
summary: Article 23 prohibits trafficking in human beings and forced labour.
hint: It is part of the Right against Exploitation.

title: 616. Employment of children below 14 years in hazardous industries is prohibited under:
summary: Article 24 prohibits child labour in hazardous employment.
hint: This Article follows the prohibition of trafficking.

title: 617. Freedom of conscience and free profession, practice and propagation of religion is guaranteed under:
summary: Article 25 guarantees freedom of religion.
hint: First Article under Right to Freedom of Religion.

title: 618. Which Article gives every religious denomination the right to manage its own religious affairs?
summary: Article 26 grants religious denominations the right to manage their own affairs.
hint: It comes immediately after Article 25.

title: 619. Which Article states that no person shall be compelled to pay taxes for the promotion of any particular religion?
summary: Article 27 prohibits taxation for promotion of a particular religion.
hint: It is related to religious taxation.

title: 620. Religious instruction cannot be provided in educational institutions wholly maintained by State funds under:
summary: Article 28 prohibits religious instruction in institutions wholly funded by the State.
hint: It is the last Article under Freedom of Religion.

title: 621. Which Article protects the interests of minorities by preserving their language, script and culture?
hint: It is the first Article under Cultural and Educational Rights.
summary: Article 29 protects the language, script and culture of minorities.

title: 622. Which Article gives minorities the right to establish and administer educational institutions?
hint: It follows Article 29.
summary: Article 30 grants minorities the right to establish and administer educational institutions.

title: 623. Which Article is known as the 'Heart and Soul of the Constitution'?
hint: Dr. B.R. Ambedkar gave this description.
summary: Article 32 is called the Heart and Soul of the Constitution.

title: 624. Which Fundamental Right is called the 'Right to Constitutional Remedies'?
hint: It empowers citizens to move the Supreme Court.
summary: Article 32 guarantees the Right to Constitutional Remedies.

title: 625. Under Article 32, citizens can directly approach:
hint: The highest court in India.
summary: Article 32 allows citizens to directly approach the Supreme Court.

title: 626. Which writ is issued to produce a person who has been illegally detained?
hint: Literally means 'produce the body'.
summary: Habeas Corpus protects personal liberty against unlawful detention.

title: 627. Which writ commands a public authority to perform its legal duty?
hint: It means 'We Command'.
summary: Mandamus directs a public authority to perform its duty.

title: 628. Which writ is issued to prevent a lower court from exceeding its jurisdiction?
hint: It means 'to forbid'.
summary: The writ of Prohibition prevents lower courts from exceeding their jurisdiction.

title: 629. Which writ is issued to quash the order of a lower court?
hint: It also transfers the case for review when appropriate.
summary: Certiorari quashes the order of an inferior court or tribunal.

title: 630. Which writ is issued to question the legal authority of a person holding a public office?
hint: It means 'By what authority?'.
summary: Quo Warranto prevents illegal occupation of a public office.

title: 631. Which court in India can issue writs under Article 32?
hint: It is the highest constitutional court.
summary: Article 32 empowers only the Supreme Court to issue writs for enforcement of Fundamental Rights.

title: 632. Under which Article can High Courts issue writs?
hint: This Article is in Part VI of the Constitution.
summary: Article 226 empowers High Courts to issue writs.

title: 633. High Courts can issue writs for:
hint: Their jurisdiction is wider than that of the Supreme Court under Article 32.
summary: High Courts can issue writs for Fundamental Rights as well as other legal rights.

options.title: Fundamental Rights and Other Legal Rights

title: 634. Which Fundamental Right is suspended during a National Emergency under Article 359 (subject to constitutional limitations)?
hint: The President suspends the right to move courts for enforcement of specified rights.
summary: The right to move courts for enforcement of specified Fundamental Rights may be suspended under Article 359.

options.title: Right to Constitutional Remedies (for specified rights)

title: 635. After the 44th Constitutional Amendment, which Articles cannot be suspended even during a National Emergency?
hint: These Articles deal with criminal protections and personal liberty.
summary: Articles 20 and 21 remain enforceable even during a National Emergency.

title: 636. Which Fundamental Right is automatically suspended during an Emergency declared on the ground of war or external aggression?
hint: It contains six freedoms.
summary: Article 19 is automatically suspended during an Emergency due to war or external aggression under Article 358.

title: 637. Which Fundamental Right is available only to Indian citizens?
hint: It includes freedom of speech and expression.
summary: The freedoms under Article 19 are available only to citizens.

title: 638. Which of the following Fundamental Rights is available to both citizens and foreigners?
hint: It guarantees equality before law.
summary: Article 14 is available to both citizens and foreigners.

title: 639. Which of the following Articles is available only to citizens of India?
hint: It prohibits discrimination on specified grounds.
summary: Article 15 applies only to citizens.

title: 640. Which of the following is NOT a Fundamental Right under the Indian Constitution?
hint: It is contained in Part IV of the Constitution.
summary: Directive Principles of State Policy are not Fundamental Rights.

title: 641. Which Part of the Constitution contains the Directive Principles of State Policy (DPSPs)?
hint: It comes immediately after Part III.
summary: Directive Principles of State Policy are contained in Part IV of the Constitution.

title: 642. Fundamental Rights in the Indian Constitution were inspired by the Constitution of:
hint: It begins with 'U'.
summary: Fundamental Rights were inspired by the U.S. Constitution.

title: 643. The concept of the 'Rule of Law' associated with Article 14 was given by:
hint: A British constitutional scholar.
summary: The concept of Rule of Law was propounded by A.V. Dicey.

title: 644. Which Fundamental Right has been described by the Supreme Court as the 'heart of fundamental rights' due to its broad interpretation?
hint: It protects life and personal liberty.
summary: Article 21 has received the widest judicial interpretation.

title: 645. Which landmark case established the 'Basic Structure Doctrine' of the Constitution?
hint: It was decided in 1973.
summary: Kesavananda Bharati v. State of Kerala established the Basic Structure Doctrine.

title: 646. Which case expanded the scope of Article 21 by establishing that 'procedure established by law' must be fair, just and reasonable?
hint: It involved the impounding of a passport.
summary: The Maneka Gandhi case revolutionized the interpretation of Article 21.

title: 647. Which Fundamental Right is available only to citizens of India?
hint: It guarantees six freedoms.
summary: Article 19 is available only to Indian citizens.

title: 648. Which Constitutional Amendment inserted Article 21A (Right to Education)?
hint: It was passed in 2002.
summary: The 86th Constitutional Amendment inserted Article 21A.

title: 649. Which Fundamental Right was described by Dr. B.R. Ambedkar as the 'Heart and Soul of the Constitution'?
hint: It allows citizens to directly approach the Supreme Court.
summary: Article 32 was described by Dr. Ambedkar as the Heart and Soul of the Constitution.

title: 650. Which of the following is NOT a Fundamental Right?
hint: It was added by the 42nd Constitutional Amendment.
summary: Fundamental Duties are contained in Part IVA and are not Fundamental Rights.
